repository.name: The-Lords-Ledger
repository.description: A medieval economic simulation game

commit.message:
Account identity layer: verification, certifications, trade caps

- Expand PlayerOrg with legal identity (legalName, dba, entityType,
  stateOfIncorporation, einLast4), NEAR wallet, certifications[],
  and reputation fields (tradeCount, totalVolumeUsdMicro, maxTradeCapMicro)
- Storage migration: loadGameState() fills in defaults for old accounts
  so existing sessions are not broken
- New API routes:
  - POST /api/verify/ein — EIN format + Middesk-ready (mock in dev,
    swap in MIDDESK_API_KEY env var for prod)
  - POST /api/verify/cert — USDA NOP live lookup + FDA format check +
    self-attested fallback for all other cert types
  - POST /api/verify/near-account — NEAR mainnet/testnet RPC account check
- New wizard step StepLegal: legal name, DBA, entity type, state of
  formation, EIN with inline verification, optional NEAR account check
- New wizard step StepCerts: add/verify/remove certifications with
  DTP-verified badge (USDA auto), self-attested badge (others), and
  expiration tracking; skippable
- SetupWizard expands from 4 to 6 steps; NEAR account optional for now
- Progressive trade caps ($5k → $15k → $50k → unlimited) enforced in
  executeTrade; cap grows automatically after each successful trade
- TradePanel shows amber warning and blocks execution when trade value
  exceeds current cap; BalanceCard shows cap + next unlock level
- EntityType and CertType enums + CERT_TYPES and TRADE_CAP_LEVELS
  constants added to types.ts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: DTP Marketplace — Direct Trade Protocol simulation game

commit.message:
Fix canvas rendering in new learning sections with double-rAF

Single requestAnimationFrame wasn't enough time for the browser to
complete layout after changing display:none to display:block. Canvas
getBoundingClientRect() returned 0x0 causing all draw functions to
bail out. Double-rAF guarantees layout is complete before measuring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

close remaining open decisions — #9, #11, #12, #13 locked

#9 watcher integrity: D (deterministic field cross-verification) always;
B (SVID-signed WatcherSummary) when SPIFFE active; standing per-admission
warning when unverified, not suppressible without explicit opt-out flag.

#11 MCP routing: Model C (federated aggregate) is the target. Coordinator
exposes single POST /mcp aggregating all admitted agents. Model B proxy
is the internal dispatch layer beneath it. Model A rejected — removes
coordinator from audit path.

#12 namespace: agent_id/tool_name as correctness floor (no collisions).
Opt-in semantic aliases via x-amf.tool_alias; collision = hard reject,
both aliases dropped, policy warning emitted.

#13 MCP call auth: three-tier model — JWT-SVID when SPIFFE active;
TOFU TLS fingerprint (SSH-style) when no SPIFFE + https; mandatory
per-call policy warning when http. AMF_MCP_REQUIRE_TLS=true blocks
plaintext MCP endpoints at admission.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add mobile responsive CSS to marketing site and video

Three breakpoints (1024px, 768px, 480px) for both pages:
- Stacked layouts, smaller fonts, collapsed nav on mobile
- Mockups resize and reflow for narrow screens
- Video controls always visible on mobile
- Full-width CTAs on phones

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reorder pipeline - apply brain mask before CC cleanup

The brain mask was applied AFTER connected component cleanup, which caused
the mask boundary to fragment large connected vessel trees into scattered
small pieces. Now the order is: threshold → resize back → brain mask → CC.

This prevents the brain mask from splitting vessel trees that were
connected in the pre-mask space.

Note: BET brain extraction is designed for structural MRI (T1/T2) and
gives incorrect results on TOF MRA data where vessels are bright and
brain parenchyma is dark. Users should skip BET for TOF MRA inputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Code <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden test fix rule: never modify tests to make code pass

Tests are the source of truth. Claude must fix the source code, not
the test. The only narrow exception is correcting a factual assertion
error in a test that was freshly generated in this same session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Wire NetworkGuard transport into web_fetch for allowlist enforcement

RegisterWebFetchTool now accepts an http.RoundTripper which is set on
the HTTP client. main.go passes netGuard.Transport("web_fetch") so all
web_fetch requests are validated against the network allowlist. Requests
to domains not in the allowlist are rejected with a "blocked by allowlist"
error before reaching the network.

commit.message:
feat: replace workspace-mcp with native Google Workspace integration via gws CLI

The third-party Python workspace-mcp server had persistent OAuth issues
(hardcoded web credential format causing "Access Blocked" errors). This
replaces it with @murph/integration-google, a new TypeScript package that
wraps Google's official gws CLI (@googleworkspace/cli).

Key changes:
- New @murph/integration-google package with GwsClient class, 24 action
  handlers across Gmail, Calendar, Tasks, Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Chat
- Email maintenance engine uses GoogleService interface (typed methods)
  instead of MCP response parsing chains
- google-auth command simplified to gws auth login (handles OAuth natively)
- Doctor checks gws binary + auth status instead of uvx/workspace-mcp
- Dashboard settings simplified (gws manages its own credentials)
- install.sh installs @googleworkspace/cli instead of workspace-mcp

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(scope): add default agent compose field to scopes (#4067)

* feat(scope): add default agent compose field to scopes

Add `defaultAgentComposeId` nullable FK on the `scopes` table to track
which agent is the default for each organization. Includes:

- DB migration with ON DELETE SET NULL constraint
- PUT /api/scopes/default-agent endpoint (admin-only, same-scope validation)
- `isDefault` boolean in compose response/list schemas
- Integration tests for the new endpoint

Closes #4065

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: combine scope query into compose join to eliminate extra db round-trip

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore missing migration snapshot and scope resolution in compose route

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: regenerate migration 0116 with drizzle-kit to fix test-migrate

The hand-written migration SQL didn't match the Drizzle schema because
the schema was missing .references(). Added FK reference to scope.ts
and regenerated migration with drizzle-kit for consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: break circular type inference in scope schema with any-pg-column

The circular import between scope.ts and agent-compose.ts caused
TypeScript to infer both table types as 'any'. Using AnyPgColumn
for the FK reference callback breaks the cycle while preserving
the FK constraint in the generated migration.

Also fixes journal timestamp ordering and regenerates the migration
with drizzle-kit for full consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add unit test fix loop to Session 2 — tests must pass before commit

npm test now runs inside the Python script before the commit. If tests
fail, Claude reviews the output and applies fixes to either the source
code or unit tests (preferring to fix the code). Up to 2 fix rounds.
If tests are still failing after max rounds, sys.exit(1) blocks the
commit entirely — a broken build never lands on the branch.

PR body now shows unit test maintenance summary and pass/fail result.

commit.message:
fix: guard WhatsApp session save against null data

PgSessionStore.save() crashed when RemoteAuth passed null/undefined
session data, violating the NOT NULL constraint on whatsapp_sessions.
This caused the session to never persist, requiring QR rescan after
every deploy. Now skips null saves and wraps in try/catch.
